summary: Migration backfills NULLs before NOT NULL ALTER; raw MODIFY preserves VARCHAR(100)/DEFAULT/COLLATE; down() reverts to nullable w/o data loss. All 4 call sites keep row-absence/DB-error guards; no new fallback. No BLOCK/WARN.

findings.claim: up() backfills NULLs before NOT NULL ALTER; ordering correct
findings.evidence: line 22 `knex('users').whereNull('timezone').update(...)` precedes line 26 raw `ALTER TABLE users MODIFY timezone VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'America/New_York' COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci`; ALTER would otherwise fail on existing NULLs

findings.claim: explicit COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci normalizes the column from the knex/MySQL-8 default; intentional, beneficial, collation-stable across up/down
findings.evidence: original col `string('timezone',100)` (20260301000000:13) carried no explicit collation -> MySQL8 default utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci; both up() and down() set utf8mb4_unicode_ci, aligning with the CLAUDE.md collation rule
findings.fix: none - note collation normalization in leg summary

findings.claim: resolveTimezone keeps try/catch DB-error guard and final return DEFAULT_TIMEZONE; only redundant `&& row.timezone` removed
findings.evidence: `if (row) return row.timezone;` inside try, `catch (_e) { /* fall through */ }`, then `return DEFAULT_TIMEZONE;` - both guards preserved; removed check is now guaranteed non-null by NOT NULL

findings.claim: MCP getUserTimezone keeps row-absence guard `user ?`, no `|| default` re-added; same pattern in schedule.js:14 and tasks.js:91
findings.evidence: `return user ? user.timezone : 'America/New_York';` with comment citing migration 20260626000000; absence of surrounding try/catch is pre-existing and unchanged by this leg

findings.claim: KnexConfigRepository.getUserTimezone correctly OUT of scope and NOT in diff; intentional null-when-unset contract (A1) and downstream || _DEFAULT_TIMEZONE guards correctly untouched
findings.evidence: line 152 maps `row && row.timezone ? row.timezone : null`; downstream guards taskMappers.js:359/387/396, schedulerSession.js:56, runSchedule.js:501 depend on that null contract and were left in place - scope discipline verified

findings.claim: migration test exists; verifying it asserts backfill-before-ALTER and up->down->up reversibility is coverage ownership
findings.evidence: test file present alongside the new migration; assertion quality is telly's column

refers.why: verify migration test asserts backfill-before-ALTER ordering and up->down->up reversibility
